Why bother writing a whole series about Hexo when there are plenty of ready-made blog platforms out there? Honestly, Hexo is one of the easiest tools to start with — you just need to grok a few basics and you can spin up your own blog pretty fast. So no more rambling, let's start trying to build your first blog from zero.

I mentioned in the intro that we're going with Hexo instead of a ready-made platform. Quick explanation: with a ready-made platform, your articles, SEO, and layout are mostly in the platform's hands, which means you can't really customise things much. The platform takes care of hosting, domain, and SEO for you — but that's also basically handing your life over to someone else.
So what does Hexo give you? Three quick wins:
But you'll need a bit of technical chops.
With some basic SEO moves and the right keywords, you can pull pretty decent traffic on your own.
Even if a third-party platform shuts down or pivots, your hard-written articles aren't going anywhere.
A few sites built with Hexo, for context:
official site is built with Hexo. (And no, this isn't a paid plug.)
So Hexo sounds great — but no upsides without downsides:
You need to find somewhere to host the site
You need to learn some command line
You need to buy a domain
Yeah, I know — you're probably ready to close this tab. To pre-empt some of that, here's the outline I'm planning for the Hexo series.
